title: dortmanna mollis
intro: Dortmanna mollis, also known as the woolly dittany, is a perennial herb native to Europe and western Asia, characterized by its small white flowers and woolly leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal effects. Consuming Dortmanna mollis has been associated with several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and salves to aid in wound healing and skin conditions.
